body_txt: A rather common stylistic choice in cartoons or other visual media is to have a character, usually a Funny Animal , wear a bow tie as an accessory . Said bow tie, which can vary in color but is usually red or black, is worn on the character in a way that it looks like it's just magically attached to their chest without falling off, especially if the character wears nothing else. Sometimes, there is a strap holding the bow tie in place, but it's small, barely visible, and only seen from certain angles, thus giving off the effect of this trope for the most part. And other times, the strap just isn't there at all regardless of which angle the character is seen from, truly making the bow tie look glued onto the character; even more so if it is small enough to not obscure their neck. All of this can be chalked up to a case of Lazy Artist . A variation of this is a hair bow being worn on a female character's head, either being laid flat on the side of the head or erected upright on the top, overall having the same effect as the tie variation. In video games, this is often justified for polygon budget reasons, as it's usually easier (and takes fewer polys) to paint a bow tie on a character model's own head texture than to extrude and shape the bow tie. Games with a customization system also tend to have the bow tie as a separate model, usually as a head accessory. On the physics side, it can be argued that customization is seldom subjected to the game's physics separately from the characters, making the trope mostly played straight. Common with Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal . No Real Life Examples, Please!
